{{Quote|And whether you pursue your civil engineering careers here or elsewhere, you'll come to rely on the DX-300 traffic package.|A lecturer on Taris|<ref name="Commencement2" />}}
The '''DX-300 traffic package''' was a traffic regulation device in use around [[3964 BBY]]. Unlike previous traffic regulation packages, the DX-300 could suggest the best track available to commuters by use of regulating signals. A lecture detailing the package was held<ref name="Commencement2" /> on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Taris/Legends|Taris]],<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic 0">[[Knights of the Old Republic 0|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 0]]</ref> during which the [[Unidentified Taris lecturer|lecturer]] claimed the device was an invaluable tool for anyone looking to work as a [[civil engineer]]. The lecture was interrupted when [[Padawan/Legends|Jedi Padawan]] [[Zayne Carrick]] crashed his [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeder]] into the building.<ref name="Commencement2" />

==Behind the scenes==
The DX-300 package's first and only mention was in [[Knights of the Old Republic 2|the second issue]] of the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Knights of the Old Republic'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series. The author of the comic, [[John Jackson Miller]], commented on his website that he found it ironic for a traffic accident to occur in the middle of a lecture about a device that handled traffic flow.<ref name="Faraway-Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #2">{{Faraway|url=comics/swknights/swkotor02.html|text=''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #2''}}</ref>
